accurate.why: All claims are supported by the bill text or are accurate context (e.g., program names and statutory citations verified).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated or contradictory claims; sponsor names, dates, and program references match the bill.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill ensures references to opioid overdose reversal drugs in HHS grant programs are not limited to naloxone.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly identifies the affected programs under section 1003 of the 21st Century Cures Act and section 516 of the Public Health Service Act.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the 'as appropriate' qualifier and limits the update requirement to references issued before enactment in the two specified programs.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the one-year update deadline and the introduction date of July 17, 2025.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures both the prospective directive and the one-year retrospective update requirement for the two named grant programs.

objective_tone.why: Summary is neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized, logically structured, and internally consistent.

concise.why: Appropriately detailed without padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: HHS, FDA, the two grant programs, statutes, sponsors, and committee are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: The one-year deadline and July 17, 2025 introduction date are stated correctly.
